title: feed.rs: html.truncate(MAX_CONTENT_LEN) panics on multi-byte UTF-8 boundaries
description: src/feed.rs:59: String::truncate() panics with 'byte index N is not a char boundary' if the byte offset 5000 falls in the middle of a multi-byte UTF-8 character (e.g., emoji, CJK, accented Latin). Because html is a Rust String (guaranteed valid UTF-8), truncating at a byte offset that is not a char boundary is a panic, not an error. This can be triggered by any feed whose entry content crosses 5000 bytes at a multi-byte character. Fix: use a helper like truncate_at_char_boundary(s, n) that walks back from n to find the last valid char boundary, or use s.char_indices().take_while(|(i, _)| *i < n).last().
close_reason: P0 fixes: AtomicU64, UTF-8 path, OsRng, 410 cleanup, constant-time key compare, checked_mul, SSRF guard, sanitized URLs, persona exists check, UTF-8 safe truncate
